slogans: Because “we downloaded it from Hugging Face” is not a threat model — it’s a release note written in advance.
Static trust issues for models that ship anyway.
We lint your billion-parameter anxiety before it becomes a calendar invite.
Your snapshot didn’t ask for a lawyer — we brought the linter.
Policy beats vibes; exit codes beat slide decks.
If your config.json is brave enough to say trust_remote_code, we are brave enough to flag it.
OSS scanners first; commercial adapters only when you opt in and write it down.
The Hub gives you bytes; we give you receipts.
Air-gapped CI is a feature, not an insult.
We scan the folder before the blameless postmortem writes itself.
Weights are data. Config is intent. Intent gets audited.
Provenance is gossip that brought a checksum to a gunfight.
Your model is not haunted; your tokenizer settings are just chatty.
“Download this and run it” is not a maturity model.
We do not ship courage; we ship manifests.
The threat model fits in git; the risk acceptance fits in your process.
If it loads arbitrary Python, it is not a minor convenience flag.
We ask rude questions quietly, in JSON.
Hermes can orchestrate; we still enforce the floor.
SBOM pointers now, excuses never.
Two packages, one contract: admit models, aggregate outcomes.
Deterministic tests, probabilistic world — we pick the tests.
The mirror is public; your policy is yours.
Ships happen; surprises should not.
